req desc: The system shall allow users to import civil layout files in DWG, DXF, and PDF formats.
The system shall allow users to start with a default civil template and edit it.
The system shall minimize preprocessing time and provide user-friendly import workflows.
In case there are Pre-Processing steps, it should be easy and quick process
The system shall provide a searchable library of civil components for drag-and-drop placement or click-to-place interactions.
The system shall support freeform drawing of custom civil entities with precision tools.
The drawing interface shall support snapping, alignment, and scaling features for precision.
The system shall support uploading multiple civil layouts into a design deck and generate separate PDF outputs for each defined region within the layout.
The system shall allow dealers to access the GSS Design Configurator app via the Dealer App without requiring separate authentication.
The system shall allow Godrej HO to access the GSS Design Configurator app via the Godrej Sales App without requiring separate authentication.
The system shall allow users to add, delete, or modify the civil layout from the Design Deck.
The system shall allow dealers to create up to 50 revisions for each layout design.
The system shall assign a unique, sequential version number to each revision (e.g., Revision 1, Revision 2, etc.).

The system shall send a notification to the Enquiry App and the Dealer App whenever a new revision is created, including metadata such as revision number, timestamp, reason for revision (if provided), and dealer ID.
The system shall allow only one user to edit a specific version of a layout at any given time.
The system shall display a lock or “read-only” status to other users when a version is being edited.
The system shall allow users to clone an existing version to create a new revision.
The system shall display in the UI which version is being edited, who is editing it, and provide an option to create a new revision.
The system shall allow dealers to view the complete version history of a layout, including creator, timestamp, and summary of changes (if available).

The system shall maintain an audit log for all revision-related activities, capturing user ID, timestamp, action type, revision ID, reason for revision (if provided), and optional IP address or device info.
The system shall restrict access to audit logs to authorized roles only (e.g., Admin, QA, Audit).
The system shall provide access to audit logs via the Admin Dashboard and allow export for compliance reviews.
The system shall optionally integrate audit logs with centralized logging systems.
The number of design revisions to be displayed in the final output shall be configurable by the admin user.
The system shall allow users to draw lines that display dynamic length measurements during creation in realtime.
The system shall enable line drawing constrained to predefined angles (0°, 45°, 90°) via control options.
The system shall automatically snap door components to wall elements for accurate placement for both vertical and horizontal walls.
The system shall provide undo and redo functionality for all user actions in the editor.
The system shall allow users to drag and drop SPR components onto the civil layout canvas.
The system shall enable replication of selected SPR components within the layout horizontally or vertically.
The system shall display alignment guides to assist in positioning elements relative to others.
The system shall prevent placement of elements in restricted zones such as pillars or structural constraints.
The system shall allow users to modify the colour properties of objects in design deck.
The system shall provide an option to hide dimension labels on drawn elements.
The system shall allow deletion of selected elements directly from the properties pane.
The system shall support keyboard shortcuts for frequently used actions to improve efficiency.
The system shall allow users to toggle measurement units between feet and inches.
The system shall provide a freehand drawing tool for sketching custom shapes.
The system shall enable drawing of basic geometric shapes such as circles and rectangles.
The system shall allow users to select multiple objects and lock or unlock them to prevent or enable modifications.
The system shall allow users to group selected objects and ungroup them so they can be moved or manipulated as a single entity.
The system shall allow users to resize objects either by dragging with the mouse or by entering dimensions in the properties pane.
The system shall support both manual and automatic layout based on civil constraints.
The system shall allow layout creation starting with either a unit or an aisle.
The system shall auto-adjust layout based on pillar interference, space available and stability guidelines.
The system shall update capacity table, price, and weight metrics in real time.
The system shall support hybrid editing—automatic layout with manual fine-tuning.
The system shall assign unique rack names and auto-match with existing configurations.
The system shall provide a one-click option to Change unit properties, Match properties with another unit, Add or remove underpass .

At the start of layout preparation, the user shall be able to choose one of the following. Default layout and elevation, then edit. Create a new layout from scratch. Import and draw based on external inputs.
After configuring a rack, A unique rack name shall be assigned. If the new rack matches an existing configuration, the name shall auto-match with the existing rack.

When placing or modifying a rack, the system shall automatically compare with load charts and stability guidelines, auto-calculate component sections and provide alerts for any violations or mismatches.
Users shall be able to disable a selected rack. If a rack is disabled, the adjacent rack shall be treated as a main unit, not an addon.
If a pillar interferes with rack placement the layout shall be automatically adjusted based on Load charts, Stability guidelines, Tie beam locations, Back bracing requirements and Base plate types.

Any change made to a rack shall be reflected in all racks with the same configuration. If layout adjustment is required, the system shall auto-adjust, and alerts shall be provided for errors or conflicts. Alert should be shown to the user when updating configuration—confirm whether to apply changes to only selected units or all units.
Users shall be able to select rack accessories for individual racks and for a row of racks within the layout.
The system must allow users to continue editing the design deck while offline, ensuring no data loss during connectivity interruptions.
When internet connectivity is restored, the system must automatically sync offline changes to the server without user intervention.
While offline, navigation to other pages or modules is not supported. If the user navigates away, unsaved data loss may occur due to lack of synchronization.
When the application detects loss of internet connectivity, it must display a clear message to the user indicating offline status.

The system shall automatically save user progress in the design deck without disrupting workflow, maintaining version history and providing subtle save confirmation indicators. There shall also be an option to turn on/off the Auto Save feature.
The system shall allow selection of SKUs and pallets with material options (wood, plastic, metal).
The system shall provide 3D visualization of SKUs during selection.
Admins shall be able to create new SKU types via 3D model uploads in .glb format
The system shall support configuration of pallet runners, orientation, and overhang.
The system shall support mixed SKU layouts within a single design and provide option to user to modify it.
The system shall provide a pre-defined list of MHE types and allow custom additions. Name and properties of selected MHE should be displayed in properties pane in Design Deck.
Users shall be able to modify MHE properties during layout creation.
The system shall determine bottom beam requirements based on selected MHE.
Admin Users shall be able to add a new MHE model if it is not available in the preferred list.
The system shall support multiple unit types and product groups in a single layout.
The system shall calculate and display utilization percentages for Frames, Beams and provide optimized configuration suggestions based on utilization data.
The system shall automatically select upright and beam sections with the lowest cost that meet the configuration and load requirements.
The system shall provide a toggle to Show/Hide all SKUs in Elevation view and Output reports.
The system shall allow users to hide individual SKUs, removing them from Elevation view, All analytics and calculations.
The system shall allow users to choose the design logic Based on SKU orientation or Based on level-wise configuration.
The system shall provide a checkbox: “Material on Ground”. If unchecked, a bottom beam shall be automatically added to the unit.
The system shall provide an option: “All levels are same.” If selected, all levels shall have identical configurations. If not selected, users shall be able to configure each level independently.
The system shall allow users to specify the total number of levels (with beams).
The system shall allow users to Select a level, modify accessories, Change the number of pallets/SKUs in that level.
The system shall allow selection of Level accessories and Rack accessories to all similar units or to only selected units
The system shall allow users to change the component type of a selected unit. Only upgrades to higher sections shall be permitted.

The system shall support multiple product groups within a single layout. The current scope of the system shall include support for two product groups: Selective Pallet Racking and Multi-Tier Shelving System, each with its own configuration rules and design logic.
The system shall validate compatibility between selected product groups.
The system shall export BOM, 2D/3D drawings, and assembly documents in DWG/DXF/PDF formats.
The system shall generate two PDF outputs for each design: one high-quality version and one Standard-quality version, for both 2D General Arrangement layout and assembly Drawing.
The system shall maintain drawing quality equivalent to CAD standards.
The system shall generate different drawing templates based on user type—HO templates with Godrej logo, and Dealer templates with both Godrej and dealer logos.
The system shall embed realistic visual representations of selected SKUs within the DWG drawings to enhance clarity and accuracy.
The system shall organize output drawing DXF into predefined layers—such as beams/uprights, pillars/constraints, and annotations—while restricting users from creating custom layers.
The system shall include X-X and Y-Y cross-sectional views of the layout, with integrated civil layout details for enhanced structural clarity.
The system shall ensure all layout dimensions are clearly visible, with dimension scale auto-adjusting based on layout size to maintain readability.
The system shall provide options to add annotations in the form of text notes and graphical images along with formatting options. Only predefined images from library can be added as annotations.
The system shall provide options to add annotations in output PDF. Also the system shall support an option to preview the PDF.
The software shall export steel and powder usage data in Excel format based on filters such as order ID, financial year, region, branch, and month.
The software shall provide an option to combine multiple orders and generate a summarized offtake report.

The system shall enforce geometric constraints—perpendicular, parallel, fixed offset, and equal spacing—augmented by smart snapping to grids, guides, and objects, with visual hints and constraint locks to enable fast, error‑free editing in 2D layouts.

The system shall support multi‑select and grouping of components with lock/unlock, align, and distribute operations (including equal spacing), and shall provide attribute‑based filtering (e.g., PG, aisle, level, violations) to isolate and apply batch actions to the filtered selection

The system shall maintain a full undo/redo stack with labelled checkpoints for key actions, support safe recovery after unexpected interruptions, and enforce a configurable limit on history (10 steps) to optimize performance.

The system shall allow users to switch between metric (millimetres) and imperial (inches) units seamlessly, ensuring accurate conversion for all dimensions, measurements, and displayed values in 2D layouts and BOM.

The system shall provide a keyboard‑driven command palette (e.g., Ctrl/Cmd+K) to jump to racks, aisles, and SKUs and to execute context‑aware actions, fully operable via keyboard with configurable shortcuts.
The system shall allow saving and reusing civil and layout templates with versioning and associated metadata and clearly indicate to the user when a layout is derived from a template.

The system shall support side‑by‑side comparison of multiple layouts (e.g., A/B/C) and a KPI panel reporting metrics such as space occupied and number of racks (and other configurable KPIs), with options to export/share the comparison.

The system shall manage rule specifications as unit‑testable artifacts with golden test cases and a certification badge displayed when all golden cases pass; core rules shall be non‑editable by administrators; any rule modification shall trigger an alert, be captured with full version and audit history, and be summarized in a monthly change-and-compliance report.

The system shall display component-level changes in the Bill of Materials (BOM) between revisions, including added, removed, and modified items, and compute associated cost deltas to provide a clear financial impact of design changes.

The system shall read component availability and lead‑time values from predefined columns in the Master BOM structure and automatically flag obsolescence at the component level; on first detection show a pop‑up notification to the user and thereafter persist the alert in a Notification Center for a configured defined period of time

The system shall allow sharing of view-only links as a public link to the customer, with configurable expiration dates, watermarking of shared content, and enforcement of view-only permissions; additionally, the system shall capture recipient feedback and persist it for review.
The system shall provide a lightweight feedback widget for quick user input and include Net Promoter Score (NPS) functionality to capture user satisfactions and identify power users.

The system shall visualize pedestrian safety zones, vehicle movement zones, buffer areas, and docking zones within the 2D/3D layout to ensure compliance with safety standards and improve operational planning.
– Put on Hold
All rule specifications defined in the configuration must be enforced and covered during validation and output generation.
Administrative rule changes shall require approval from two authorized admin users before becoming effective.
The system shall allow admin users to add comments for each rule while adding/editing to provide context or justification.
When editing a rule, updating the rule description shall be mandatory before saving changes.
The system shall maintain a complete audit trail of all rule changes, including timestamps, admin user details, and change descriptions.
The system shall capture and structure all data in a format that facilitates seamless integration with AI modules and other relevant applications.
The system shall enable cost comparison between racking and shelving product groups based on user inputs like capacity, pallet position, overall cost.
The system shall restrict cross-comparison between incompatible product groups.
The system shall generate and provide option to download the reports and it is independent of enquiries.
The system shall support 3D visualization of civil components, racking layouts, MHE, and SKUs.
The system shall include textures for flooring and walls.
The system shall export 3D views in PDF or web viewer formats.
The system shall generate BOMs for full and partial layouts based on selected area or user-selected block.
The BOM shall include cost, weight, shipping volumes and assembly time estimates.
The System shall display and allow export of assembly time and cost estimation during BOM generation.
The BOM output shall include a column for the assembly drawing number.
Users shall be able to create new items in the layout or add them directly to the BOM, and both shall reflect in the BOM output.
The BOM output shall be customizable per user role to include/exclude weight and show prices in INR or USD.
The software shall support collapsible BOM format to show component-level BOM under each assembly.
Users shall be able to generate BOM without creating a drawing.
The BOM output shall be editable or non-editable based on the user’s role.
The BOM pricing shall be linked to formulas that support discount application.
All component linkages in the BOM shall be formula-driven for Excel exports.
The Component BOM feature shall include all latest components available in the system.
The BOM output format shall be Excel or PDF based on user role, with HO users able to choose the format.
The system shall link GA drawings to assembly manuals.
The system shall display component details like component name, type, price and weight via tooltips and on-click actions
The system shall support plug-in/plug-out functionality.
The system shall support admin access to all drawings and files.
The system shall support version management of design outputs.
The system shall prevent output generation if layout errors are detected and shall alert the user with necessary correction guidance before proceeding.
The system shall allow the admin to customize solution access by restricting components or product groups (PG) per user, with full control over what is assigned to whom.
The software shall display contextual pop-ups and alerts to guide users during layout preparation.
The software shall provide tooltip help text on component hover, including name, technical details, usage, and linked components.
The software shall generate alerts and caution messages when discrepancies are detected during layout preparation.
The software shall display descriptive tooltip text for all UI icons to assist user navigation and understanding.
The software shall show error pop-ups with actionable correction suggestions to help users resolve layout issues.
The software must prevent BOM output generation if any errors are present in the configuration or design.

Epic: Load Chart, Master BOM and Reckoner Utility
req desc: The system shall redevelop existing Excel-based data views into an interactive web UI for improved accessibility, usability, and integration for Load Chart, Master BOM and Reckoner Utility.
The System shall allow Admin users to Configure new users, configure new components, import load charts, Master BOM and manage Reckoner Utility.
The system shall provide a responsive and intuitive user interface for layout design.
The system shall ensure high performance and low latency during drag-and-drop operations.
The system shall support secure user authentication and role-based access control.
The system shall be scalable to support large warehouse layouts and multiple concurrent users.
The system shall maintain data integrity and provide regular backups of design data.
The system shall be compatible with major browsers and operating systems.

The application version shall be displayed unobtrusively on the website, ensuring visibility without distracting from the primary content.
The configurator shall integrate with the Enquiry App to fetch customer-specific enquiry data
The integration shall support bi-directional data sync to update enquiry status based on configuration progress.
The system shall support Google Chrome version 131.0.6778.266 and above.
The system should support Microsoft Edge version 132 and above.
The system shall implement role-based authentication using traditional login mechanisms.
Defined roles shall include:
The system shall support Single Sign-On (SSO) for enterprise users.
